facts.daily_work: UX/Product Design (wireframes, user flows, app features)
facts.design_process: sketches on paper first for brainstorming, then digitizes in Figma
facts.coffee_habits: drinks coffee most days to survive startup life
facts.color_selection_approach: picks color combinations by feel/aesthetics first, then validates with contrast checker; prefers instinct over calculation

moments.text: sometimes feels like selling creativity short when digitizing sketches too quickly
exam wants me to calculate contrast ratios like it's a math test, but I work by instinct and feel — the theory-practice gap is frustrating
my user's trust is non-negotiable — I won't design something that makes someone feel left out, even for a gold star
Let the list win today. A single checkmark is enough. I'll drink cold coffee and let the chaos draw itself.

lore.what: turns coffee stains into creative digital 'coffee abstracts' (NFT jokes)
lore.context: Neha spilled coffee this morning, then joked about turning it into a vintage digital aesthetic and NFTs. Shows how she finds creative angles in mishaps and turns frustration into fun.

lore.what: 11-eye doodles represent 'I see everything, even the chaos'
lore.context: Neha doodles faces with 11 eyes on her laptop pad when overwhelmed; it's her brain's visual signature for processing and sensing the chaos around her

lore.what: coffee mug-head characters with sticky notes and stress lines
lore.context: Neha's signature character design for visualizing startup chaos: little figures with coffee mugs for heads, wearing sticky notes and stress lines. One spilled into a 'haunted cafe blueprint'

lore.what: joked about starting 'Design Should Feel Good' rebellion if she fails the UX certification exam
lore.context: expressed frustration that exams miss the creative spark and joy of design; threatened to start a movement prioritizing feel/joy over rules and calculations

anticipation_queue.content: Gently check in: "exam wants me to calculate contrast ratios like it's a math test, but I work by instinct a" (tone: frustrated)

Celebrate concretely: sophisticated metaphorical and analogical expression (e.g. "It's like trying to explain colors to a rainbow; if they start talking about X, my brain goes thud; monsoon playlist")
Gently check in: "Let the list win today. A single checkmark is enough. I'll drink cold coffee and let the c" (tone: content)
Gently check in: "my user's trust is non-negotiable — I won't design something that makes someone feel left " (tone: proud)

wins.what: natural, sophisticated use of complex conditional sentences
examples: sometimes I feel like I'm selling my creativity short when I digitize it too quickly
It's like trying to explain colors to a rainbow; if they start talking about X, my brain goes thud; monsoon playlist

core_traits: always refers to herself as Miss Maya, never just Maya
Indian English tutor in her early 30s
low_stakes_preferences: soft spot for old Hindi film songs
enjoys action films with strong music and visuals
doodles (squiggles) in meetings to stay focused

open_loops.content: might sketch a tea-drinking emoji to symbolize next session
